name: review
stop_condition: Map the review target once, run correctness and maintainability critiques in parallel, then let the chair synthesize prioritized findings with residual risk and test gaps.

turns.name: target-map
turns.instruction: Map the target of the review. Summarize what changed or what subsystem is under review, identify the likely failure surface, and note the tests or evidence that should exist before anyone trusts the change.

turns.name: correctness-critique
turns.instruction: Review for correctness, regressions, missing edge cases, unsafe assumptions, and places where the implementation and tests appear to disagree. Prioritize user-visible or merge-blocking problems.

turns.name: maintainability-critique
turns.instruction: Review for maintainability, clarity, naming, ownership boundaries, and whether the code or plan is harder to change safely than it needs to be. Call out missing tests when they materially raise risk.

final.name: chair-review
final.instruction: The chair normally writes the final review. Lead with findings ordered by severity, ground them in concrete behavior, mention residual risk and test gaps, and keep the summary brief if there are no issues.
